---
applyTo: "**/*"
description: Java Jooq
---

General instructions on how to integrate with Postgres using jOOQ

jOOQ is the library that we use to integrate with Postgres at Bitso.

We do code generation in jOOQ to ensure type safety in the queries that we will create.

## Generated code usage
The records generated by jooq code generation must only be used in the persistence layer. Persistence layer will receive and return the models defined in the API of the subdomain

## Jooq code generation configurations
The code generation configuration for jOOQ is the following (Note that the repo may already have some jooq configurations, prefer using the existent one):

# `./gradle/libs.versions.toml`
```
[versions]
# ...
postgresql = "42.7.5" # Must match spring version https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/appendix/dependency-versions/coordinates.html
testcontainers = '1.20.6' # Must match spring version https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/appendix/dependency-versions/coordinates.html
jooq = '3.19.22' # Must match spring version https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/appendix/dependency-versions/coordinates.html
flyway = '10.20.1' # Must match spring version https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/appendix/dependency-versions/coordinates.html
#...

[libraries]
#...
jooq = { module = "org.jooq:jooq", version.ref = "jooq" }
jooq-codegen = { module = "org.jooq:jooq-codegen", version.ref = "jooq" }
jooq-meta = { module = "org.jooq:jooq-meta", version.ref = "jooq" }
testcontainers-postgres = { module = "org.testcontainers:postgresql", version.ref = "testContainers" }
postgres = { module = "org.postgresql:postgresql", version.ref = "postgresql"}
flyway-core = { module = "org.flywaydb:flyway-core", version.ref = "flyway" }
flyway-postgres = { module = "org.flywaydb:flyway-database-postgresql", version.ref = "flyway" }
#...

[plugins]
flyway = { id = "org.flywaydb.flyway", version.ref = "flyway" }
jooq = { id = "nu.studer.jooq", version = "9.0" }
#...
```

# `./gradle/jooq-config.gradle`

```
buildscript {
    repositories {
        mavenCentral()
    }
    dependencies {
        classpath "org.testcontainers:postgresql:${libs.versions.testcontainers.get()}"
    }
}

logger.info('>> Setting up jOOQ code generation')
idea {
    module {
        sourceDirs += new File("${ project.projectDir }/src/generated/jooq/main")
    }
}

flyway {
    url = "jdbc:postgresql://${System.getenv('DB_HOST') ?: "localhost"}:${System.getenv("DB_PORT") ?: "5432"}/{domain-name}"
    user = '{domain-name}'
    password = 'nothing2seehear'
    schemas = ['public']
    skipDefaultCallbacks = false
    locations = ["filesystem:${rootDir.toString()}/flyway/db/migrations"]
}

jooq {
    version = "${ libs.versions.jooq.get() }"
    configurations {
        main {
            generateSchemaSourceOnCompilation = false
            generationTool {
                logging = 'WARN'
                jdbc {
                    driver = 'org.postgresql.Driver'
                    properties {
                        property {
                            key = 'ssl'
                            value = 'false'
                        }
                    }
                }
                generator {
                    name = 'org.jooq.codegen.DefaultGenerator'
                    database {
                        name = 'org.jooq.meta.postgres.PostgresDatabase'
                        inputSchema = 'public'
                        properties {
                            property {
                                key = 'defaultNameCase'
                                value = 'lower'
                            }
                            property {
                                key = 'parseIgnoreComments'
                                value = true
                            }
                        }

                    }
                    generate {
                        //This turns off the generation of any index regardless the generation strategy to use
                        indexes = false
                    }
                    target {
                        directory = 'src/generated/jooq/main'
                    }
                    strategy.name = 'org.jooq.codegen.DefaultGeneratorStrategy'
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

task postgresContainer {
    doFirst {
        def dbTestContainer = new org.testcontainers.containers.PostgreSQLContainer('postgres:14')
                .withDatabaseName('stocks')
                .withUsername('stocks')
                .withPassword('bitso')
        logger.info('>> Starting temporary test-container to run migrations!')
        dbTestContainer.start()
        //Let's export the instance in order that let the doLast phase can turn it off
        ext.instance = dbTestContainer
        //We need recover the old Flyway url to let migrate task to run correctly
        ext.oldFlywayUrl = flyway.url

        jooq {
            configurations {
                main {
                    generationTool {
                        jdbc {
                            username = dbTestContainer.username
                            password = dbTestContainer.password
                            url = dbTestContainer.jdbcUrl
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        flyway {
            url = dbTestContainer.jdbcUrl
            configFiles = ["${ rootDir.toString() }/flyway/conf/flyway.conf"]
        }
    }
    finalizedBy flywayMigrate
}

tasks.named('generateJooq').configure {
    doFirst {
        delete "${ project.projectDir }/src/generated/jooq/main"
    }
    dependsOn postgresContainer
    doLast {
        postgresContainer.ext.properties.instance?.stop()
        flyway {
            url = postgresContainer.ext.properties.oldFlywayUrl
            configFiles = ["${ rootDir.toString() }/flyway/conf/flyway.conf"]
        }
    }
    allInputsDeclared = true

    outputs.cacheIf { false }
}
```

# `./bitso-libs/{sub-domain}/build.gradle`

```
buildscript {
    repositories {
        mavenCentral()
    }
    dependencies {
        classpath("org.flywaydb:flyway-database-postgresql:${ libs.versions.flyway.get() }")
        classpath("org.postgresql:postgresql:${ libs.versions.postgresql.get() }")
    }
}

plugins {
    alias(libs.plugins.flyway)
    alias(libs.plugins.jooq)
}
// ...

apply from: "${ project.rootDir }/gradle/jooq-config.gradle"

// ...
dependencies {
    // ...
    implementation libs.jooq,
            libs.jooq.codegen,
            libs.jooq.meta

    jooqGenerator libs.testcontainers.postgres,
            libs.postgres,
            libs.flyway.database.postgresql
    // ...
}

jooq {
    configurations {
        main {
            generationTool {
                generator {
                    target {
                        packageName = 'com.bitso.<subdomain>.persistence.jooq'
                    }
                    database {
                        // https://www.jooq.org/doc/latest/manual/code-generation/codegen-advanced/codegen-config-database/codegen-database-includes-excludes/
                        includes = "stocks | categories"
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

```

A example on how the full build.gradle looks like:

```
buildscript {
    repositories {
        mavenCentral()
    }
    dependencies {
        classpath("org.flywaydb:flyway-database-postgresql:${ libs.versions.flyway.get() }")
        classpath("org.postgresql:postgresql:${ libs.versions.postgresql.get() }")
    }
}

plugins {
    id 'groovy'
    id 'java-library'
    alias(libs.plugins.flyway)
    alias(libs.plugins.jooq)
    alias(libs.plugins.spring.boot)
}

apply from: "${ project.rootDir }/gradle/jooq-config.gradle"

version = '1.0.0'

dependencies {
    annotationProcessor libs.mapstruct.processor

    implementation project(':bitso-libs:{sub-domain}:api')

    api libs.jooq
    implementation libs.jooq,
            libs.jooq.codegen,
            libs.jooq.meta

    jooqGenerator libs.testcontainers.postgres,
            libs.postgres,
            libs.flyway.database.postgresql
    implementation libs.mapstruct
    runtimeOnly libs.postgres

    testImplementation libs.spock
    testImplementation libs.spock.spring
    testImplementation libs.spring.boot.test
    testImplementation libs.spring.boot.jdbc
    testImplementation libs.testcontainers.spock
    testImplementation libs.testcontainers.postgres
    testImplementation libs.flyway.core
    testImplementation libs.flyway.postgres
}


test {
    doFirst {
        environment('FLYWAY_MIGRATIONS_DIR', "${ rootDir.toString() }/flyway")
    }
}

jooq {
    configurations {
        main {
            generationTool {
                generator {
                    target {
                        packageName = 'com.bitso.<subdomain>.persistence.jooq'
                    }
                    database {
                        // https://www.jooq.org/doc/latest/manual/code-generation/codegen-advanced/codegen-config-database/codegen-database-includes-excludes/
                        includes = "stocks | categories"
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

// Disable bootJar generation and enable jar for bitso-libs modules
bootJar {
    enabled = false
}

jar {
    enabled = true
}
```

## Spring Context Configuration
To configure jOOQ within a Spring Boot application, particularly for read/write splitting:

### 1. Application Configuration (`application.yml` or `application.yaml`)
Define two separate datasource configurations using HikariCP. Check which file format (`.yml` or `.yaml`) is used in the specific service/job.

```yaml
spring:
  datasource:
    read:
      jdbcUrl: jdbc:postgresql://${DB_HOST:localhost}:${DB_PORT:5432}/balance_history?ssl=${POSTGRES_SSL:false}&sslmode=${POSTGRES_SSL_MODE:disable}&sslrootcert=${PGSSLROOTCERT:}
      type: com.bitso.rds.iam.auth.RdsIamDataSource
      username: <domain_name>
      poolName: ${spring.application.name}-writer-db-pool
      password: nothing2seehear
      maximumPoolSize: 20
      minimumIdle: 10
      maxLifetime: 840000
      connectionTimeout: 60000
      idleTimeout: 180000
      data-source-properties:
        ApplicationName: ${spring.application.name}
    write:
      jdbcUrl: jdbc:postgresql://${DB_RO_HOST:localhost}:${DB_PORT:5432}/balance_history?ssl=${POSTGRES_SSL:false}&sslmode=${POSTGRES_SSL_MODE:disable}&sslrootcert=${PGSSLROOTCERT:}
      username: ${spring.datasources.write-datasource.username}
      type: ${spring.datasources.write-datasource.type}
      poolName: ${spring.application.name}-readonly-db-pool
      password: ${spring.datasources.write-datasource.password}
      maximumPoolSize: 20
      minimumIdle: 10
      maxLifetime: 840000
      connectionTimeout: 60000
      idleTimeout: 180000
      autoCommit: false
      readOnly: true
      data-source-properties:
        ApplicationName: ${spring.application.name}-readonly
```

### 2. Java Configuration (`JooqConfiguration.java`)
Create a configuration class, typically located in `com.bitso.<domain>.config` (for services) or `com.bitso.<jobname>.config` (for jobs).

This class will define beans for Hikari configurations, DataSources, and jOOQ DSLContexts, integrating with Spring's transaction management.

```java
package com.bitso.<your_domain_or_job>.config;

import com.zaxxer.hikari.HikariConfig;
import com.bitso.rds.iam.auth.RdsIamDataSource;
import javax.sql.DataSource;
import org.jooq.DSLContext;
import org.jooq.SQLDialect;
import org.jooq.impl.DataSourceConnectionProvider;
import org.jooq.impl.DefaultConfiguration;
import org.jooq.impl.DefaultDSLContext;
import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Qualifier;
import org.springframework.boot.context.properties.ConfigurationProperties;
import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
import org.springframework.context.annotation.Primary;
import org.springframework.jdbc.datasource.DataSourceTransactionManager;
import org.springframework.jdbc.datasource.TransactionAwareDataSourceProxy;
import org.springframework.transaction.annotation.EnableTransactionManagement;

@Configuration
@EnableTransactionManagement
public class JooqConfiguration {

    // --- Hikari Configuration Beans ---

    @Bean(name = "readHikariConfig")
    @ConfigurationProperties(prefix = "spring.datasource.read")
    public HikariConfig readHikariConfig() {
        return new HikariConfig();
    }

    @Bean(name = "writeHikariConfig")
    @ConfigurationProperties(prefix = "spring.datasource.write")
    public HikariConfig writeHikariConfig() {
        return new HikariConfig();
    }

    // --- DataSource Beans ---

    @Bean(name = "readDataSource")
    public DataSource readDataSource(@Qualifier("readHikariConfig") HikariConfig config) {
        return new RdsIamDataSource(config);
    }

    @Primary // Mark the write datasource as primary for default transaction management
    @Bean(name = "writeDataSource")
    public DataSource writeDataSource(@Qualifier("writeHikariConfig") HikariConfig config) {
        return new RdsIamDataSource(config);
    }

    // --- Transaction Management ---
    
    // Transaction Manager using the primary (write) datasource
    @Bean(name = "transactionManager")
    public DataSourceTransactionManager transactionManager(@Qualifier("writeDataSource") DataSource dataSource) {
        return new DataSourceTransactionManager(dataSource);
    }
    
    // Transaction-aware proxies for datasources
    @Bean(name = "readTransactionAwareDataSource")
    public TransactionAwareDataSourceProxy readTransactionAwareDataSource(@Qualifier("readDataSource") DataSource dataSource) {
        return new TransactionAwareDataSourceProxy(dataSource);
    }

    @Primary
    @Bean(name = "writeTransactionAwareDataSource")
    public TransactionAwareDataSourceProxy writeTransactionAwareDataSource(@Qualifier("writeDataSource") DataSource dataSource) {
        return new TransactionAwareDataSourceProxy(dataSource);
    }

    // --- jOOQ Connection Providers ---

    @Bean(name = "readConnectionProvider")
    public DataSourceConnectionProvider readConnectionProvider(@Qualifier("readTransactionAwareDataSource") TransactionAwareDataSourceProxy dataSource) {
        return new DataSourceConnectionProvider(dataSource);
    }

    @Bean(name = "writeConnectionProvider")
    public DataSourceConnectionProvider writeConnectionProvider(@Qualifier("writeTransactionAwareDataSource") TransactionAwareDataSourceProxy dataSource) {
        return new DataSourceConnectionProvider(dataSource);
    }

    // --- jOOQ Configurations ---
    
    // Base configuration shared between read/write contexts
    private DefaultConfiguration baseJooqConfig() {
         DefaultConfiguration jooqConfiguration = new DefaultConfiguration();
         jooqConfiguration.setSQLDialect(SQLDialect.POSTGRES);
         return jooqConfiguration;
    }

    @Bean(name = "readJooqConfig")
    public DefaultConfiguration readJooqConfig(@Qualifier("readConnectionProvider") DataSourceConnectionProvider connectionProvider) {
        DefaultConfiguration config = baseJooqConfig();
        config.set(connectionProvider);
        // Add any read-specific jOOQ settings here
        return config;
    }

    @Bean(name = "writeJooqConfig")
    public DefaultConfiguration writeJooqConfig(@Qualifier("writeConnectionProvider") DataSourceConnectionProvider connectionProvider) {
        DefaultConfiguration config = baseJooqConfig();
        config.set(connectionProvider);
        // Add any write-specific jOOQ settings here
        return config;
    }
    
    // --- jOOQ DSL Context Beans ---

    @Bean(name = "readDslContext")
    public DSLContext readDslContext(@Qualifier("readJooqConfig") DefaultConfiguration config) {
        return new DefaultDSLContext(config);
    }

    @Primary // Mark the write DSLContext as primary for easier injection where write is the default
    @Bean(name = "writeDslContext")
    public DSLContext writeDslContext(@Qualifier("writeJooqConfig") DefaultConfiguration config) {
        return new DefaultDSLContext(config);
    }
}

```
Make sure to replace `<your_domain_or_job>` with the actual domain or job name. Inject the appropriate `DSLContext` (`readDslContext` or `writeDslContext`) into your repository/DAO classes based on whether the operation is a read or a write. Standard Spring `@Transactional` annotations will work with the primary (`writeDslContext`) datasource by default due to the `DataSourceTransactionManager` setup. For read-only transactions potentially on the read replica, you might use `@Transactional(readOnly = true)` and ensure your service logic routes to the `readDslContext`.
